articles.headline: Government Unveils 'Atmanirbhar Krishi Abhiyan 2.0' to Boost Agricultural Exports and Farmer Income
articles.explainer:
• The Union Agriculture Ministry launched 'Atmanirbhar Krishi Abhiyan 2.0', a comprehensive policy framework aimed at enhancing agricultural productivity, promoting value addition, and increasing farmer incomes.
• The initiative focuses on integrating advanced technologies like AI and blockchain for supply chain management, improving access to credit for farmers, and strengthening agricultural infrastructure.
• A key component is the establishment of 'Agri-Export Zones' (AEZs) and dedicated logistics corridors to facilitate seamless export of Indian agricultural produce to global markets.
• The program also includes provisions for skill development and capacity building among farmers, particularly in sustainable farming practices and digital literacy.
• It aims to reduce post-harvest losses significantly and ensure better price realization for farmers by connecting them directly to national and international buyers.

articles.headline: Supreme Court Committee Recommends AI Integration for Faster Dispute Resolution in Lower Courts
articles.explainer:
• A Supreme Court-appointed committee has submitted a report recommending the phased integration of Artificial Intelligence (AI) tools to assist in dispute resolution processes, particularly in district and subordinate courts.
• The recommendations include using AI for case management, identifying similar precedents, automating administrative tasks, and transcribing court proceedings, aiming to reduce pendency.
• The committee emphasized that AI would serve as an assistive tool for judges and not replace human judgment, focusing on improving efficiency and access to justice.
• Concerns regarding data privacy, algorithmic bias, and digital divide were also addressed, with proposals for robust data protection frameworks and ethical AI guidelines.
• The report suggests pilot projects in select high courts and district courts before a nationwide rollout, along with extensive training programs for judicial officers and legal professionals.

articles.headline: India and Sri Lanka Conclude Joint Naval Exercise 'SLINEX 2026' in Bay of Bengal
articles.explainer:
• The 14th edition of the bilateral naval exercise 'SLINEX 2026' between India and Sri Lanka concluded in the Bay of Bengal, focusing on enhancing interoperability and mutual understanding.
• The exercise included advanced anti-submarine warfare drills, maritime surveillance operations, search and rescue scenarios, and cross-deck flying operations.
• Both navies exchanged best practices in maritime security, disaster relief, and humanitarian assistance operations, reinforcing regional stability.
• The exercise underscored the strong maritime security cooperation between the two nations and their commitment to a free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific region.
• Senior naval officials from both countries reiterated their resolve to counter common maritime threats, including piracy, illegal fishing, and drug trafficking.

articles.headline: National Quantum Mission Reports Significant Progress in Quantum Computing and Communication
articles.explainer:
• The Department of Science & Technology (DST) released a progress report on the National Quantum Mission (NQM), highlighting advancements in developing quantum computing platforms and secure quantum communication networks.
• Researchers have achieved milestones in building indigenous quantum processors, with promising results in error correction and qubit stability, crucial for practical quantum applications.
• The mission has also made strides in quantum key distribution (QKD) technology, aiming to establish secure communication links for critical infrastructure and defense.
• Collaborative projects between academic institutions, industry partners, and government labs are accelerating research and development, fostering a robust quantum ecosystem in India.
• The NQM is positioning India as a global leader in quantum technologies, with a focus on both fundamental research and application development in areas like healthcare, finance, and defense.

articles.headline: ISRO Study Highlights Worsening Urban Heat Island Effect in Indian Cities
articles.explainer:
• A new study by the Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) utilizing satellite imagery and climate models has revealed a significant increase in the Urban Heat Island (UHI) effect across major Indian cities.
• The research indicates that rapid urbanization, reduction in green cover, and extensive use of heat-absorbing construction materials are primary contributors to higher temperatures in urban areas compared to their rural surroundings.
• Cities like Delhi, Mumbai, and Chennai are experiencing UHI intensities ranging from 3-7°C, leading to increased energy consumption for cooling, adverse health impacts, and exacerbated air pollution.
• The study emphasizes the urgent need for urban planning strategies that incorporate green infrastructure, cool roofs, and sustainable building materials to mitigate the UHI effect.
• ISRO's findings provide crucial data for policymakers to develop targeted interventions for climate resilience and sustainable urban development.

articles.headline: India Hosts G20+ Summit on Global Digital Public Infrastructure Framework

• The summit aims to standardize protocols and foster interoperability for DPIs like digital identity, payments, and data exchange across member nations, promoting inclusive digital transformation globally.
• Discussions include financing mechanisms for DPI adoption in developing countries, capacity building, and addressing concerns related to data governance and cross-border data flows.
• India's experience with Aadhaar, UPI, and other DPIs is being showcased as a model for scalable and inclusive digital solutions.
• The initiative seeks to bridge the global digital divide and enable equitable access to digital services for billions worldwide.

articles.headline: Archaeological Survey of India Unearths Ancient Buddhist Monastery in Odisha
articles.explainer:
• The Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) has announced the discovery of a sprawling ancient Buddhist monastic complex during excavations in the Jajpur district of Odisha.
• The site, believed to date back to the 7th-10th century CE, includes stupas, meditation cells, intricate sculptures of Buddhist deities, and a well-preserved prayer hall.
• This discovery sheds new light on the spread of Vajrayana Buddhism in Eastern India and the rich cultural heritage of ancient Kalinga.
• Artifacts recovered from the site, including terracotta seals and pottery, provide valuable insights into the daily life and religious practices of the monastic community.
• The ASI plans further excavations and conservation efforts, aiming to develop the site as a major heritage tourism destination and a center for Buddhist studies.

articles.headline: National Policy on Climate Resilience and Adaptation Unveiled, Focus on Vulnerable Regions
articles.explainer:
• The Union Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change has launched a comprehensive 'National Policy on Climate Resilience and Adaptation' to address the growing impacts of climate change in India.
• The policy prioritizes vulnerable regions and communities, focusing on building climate-resilient infrastructure, promoting sustainable agriculture, and strengthening early warning systems for extreme weather events.
• Key strategies include mainstreaming climate adaptation into development planning, enhancing ecosystem-based adaptation, and mobilizing climate finance from domestic and international sources.
• It emphasizes multi-stakeholder collaboration, involving state governments, local bodies, civil society, and the private sector, for effective implementation.
• The policy aims to reduce climate-related risks, protect livelihoods, and ensure sustainable development in the face of a changing climate.

articles.headline: ISRO Gears Up for Venus Orbiter Mission 'Shukrayaan-2' Launch Window
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) has announced that preparations are in full swing for the 'Shukrayaan-2' mission, India's second planetary mission to Venus.
• The mission aims to study Venus's atmosphere, surface, and subsurface, focusing on its geological processes, atmospheric composition, and the presence of any active volcanism.
• Shukrayaan-2 will carry advanced scientific payloads, including a synthetic aperture radar (SAR) for high-resolution mapping of the Venusian surface and instruments to analyze atmospheric gases.
• The launch window is anticipated in the latter half of 2026, leveraging optimal planetary alignment for a cost-effective trajectory.
• This mission reinforces India's growing capabilities in deep space exploration and its commitment to contributing to global planetary science research.

articles.headline: Government Launches 'Saksham Yuva' Skill Development Program for Rural Youth
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Skill Development and Entrepreneurship, in collaboration with state governments, has launched the 'Saksham Yuva' program, a new initiative aimed at empowering rural youth through skill development and vocational training.
• The program focuses on demand-driven skills relevant to local economies, including renewable energy installation, digital literacy, sustainable agriculture practices, and rural tourism.
• It integrates apprenticeship opportunities with industry partners and provides financial assistance for entrepreneurship, encouraging self-employment among beneficiaries.
• 'Saksham Yuva' seeks to bridge the skill gap in rural areas, enhance employability, and reduce distress migration to urban centers.
• The initiative includes provisions for special training modules for women and marginalized communities, ensuring inclusive growth and gender equality in skill development.

articles.headline: Election Commission Proposes Amendments to Enhance Transparency in Electoral Bonds Scheme
articles.explainer:
• The Election Commission of India (ECI) has submitted a proposal to the Law Ministry suggesting amendments to the Electoral Bonds Scheme to enhance transparency and accountability in political funding.
• Key recommendations include mandating disclosure of the identity of electoral bond purchasers to the ECI, while maintaining anonymity for political parties.
• The ECI also proposed reducing the anonymity period for the bonds and increasing the frequency of their issuance windows to ensure more regular and transparent funding cycles.
• These proposals aim to address long-standing concerns raised by civil society organizations and various political parties regarding the opacity of the current scheme.
• The suggested changes are intended to strike a balance between donor privacy and public right to information regarding political funding.

articles.headline: India Calls for Review of Indus Waters Treaty Provisions Amidst Climate Change Impacts
articles.explainer:
• India has formally called for a review of certain provisions of the Indus Waters Treaty (IWT) with Pakistan, citing the changing hydrological conditions due to climate change and increasing water demands.
• The Ministry of Water Resources highlighted that the existing treaty, signed in 1960, may not adequately address contemporary challenges such as glacial melt, altered river flows, and growing population needs.
• India seeks to explore mechanisms for better water management, flood control, and optimal utilization of western river waters allocated to Pakistan, while respecting the spirit of the treaty.
• This move follows ongoing expert-level discussions and aims to initiate a constructive dialogue with Pakistan on updating the treaty to reflect current realities.
• The IWT, brokered by the World Bank, has largely remained resilient despite political tensions, governing the sharing of six rivers between the two nations.

articles.headline: Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission Reports Accelerated Adoption of Digital Health IDs
articles.explainer:
• The National Health Authority (NHA) announced a significant surge in the creation of Ayushman Bharat Health Accounts (ABHA IDs), indicating accelerated adoption of the Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM).
• Over 500 million unique digital health IDs have now been generated, enabling citizens to link their health records digitally and access various healthcare services.
• The mission aims to create a seamless online platform for interoperability of health data, improving efficiency, transparency, and accessibility of healthcare across India.
• Integration with public and private healthcare providers, including hospitals, clinics, and diagnostic labs, is expanding rapidly, forming a robust digital health ecosystem.

articles.headline: NITI Aayog Proposes Comprehensive Circular Economy Policy Framework

• The framework identifies key sectors for intervention, including electronics, batteries, vehicles, packaging, and construction & demolition waste, proposing specific strategies for each.
• It emphasizes Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR), fostering innovation in recycling technologies, and creating market demand for recycled products.
• The policy aims to reduce India's reliance on virgin resources, mitigate environmental pollution, and create new economic opportunities in the recycling and reuse sectors.

articles.headline: RBI Report Highlights Progress in Financial Inclusion, Focus on Digital Payments

• While urban areas show high financial literacy and service utilization, the report highlights the need for targeted interventions to improve financial inclusion in remote rural areas and among marginalized communities.

articles.headline: Conservation Efforts Boost Ganges River Dolphin Population in Key Habitats
articles.explainer:
• Recent surveys conducted by the Wildlife Institute of India (WII) and state forest departments indicate a positive trend in the population of the endangered Ganges River Dolphin in several protected areas.
• Increased enforcement against illegal fishing, reduced pollution levels in critical stretches of the Ganges and its tributaries, and community engagement in conservation efforts are cited as primary reasons for the rebound.
• The 'Project Dolphin' initiative, launched by the central government, has played a crucial role in coordinating these conservation activities, including habitat restoration and awareness campaigns.
• Despite progress, challenges remain from habitat fragmentation due to dams, plastic pollution, and occasional accidental deaths from fishing gear, necessitating continuous monitoring and intervention.
• The Ganges River Dolphin, India's National Aquatic Animal, serves as an indicator species for the health of the riverine ecosystem.

articles.headline: Government Amends E-Waste Management Rules to Strengthen Collection and Recycling Targets
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change has notified amendments to the E-Waste (Management) Rules, 2022, introducing stricter targets and expanding the scope for collection and recycling of electronic waste.
• The revised rules mandate higher Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR) obligations for manufacturers and importers, requiring them to collect and recycle a larger percentage of their products.
• A new digital portal for tracking e-waste generation and processing has been launched, aiming for greater transparency and accountability across the value chain.
• The amendments also include provisions for promoting refurbishment and reuse of electronic products, thereby extending their lifecycle and reducing the burden on raw materials.
• These measures are expected to formalize the informal e-waste sector, create green jobs, and mitigate the environmental and health hazards associated with improper e-waste disposal.

Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.

mainsPoints: Discuss the challenges posed by rapid generation of e-waste in India and the importance of robust regulatory frameworks for its management.

Evaluate the role of Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR) in promoting sustainable consumption and production patterns in the electronics sector.

articles.trivia: India is one of the top five e-waste generating countries in the world, with a significant portion of it managed by the informal sector.

articles.headline: Ministry of Ports, Shipping and Waterways Reviews Progress of Maritime India Vision 2030
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Ports, Shipping and Waterways held a high-level review meeting to assess the progress of initiatives under the 'Maritime India Vision 2030'.
• The vision aims to transform the Indian maritime sector by boosting port infrastructure, promoting coastal shipping, enhancing shipbuilding capabilities, and fostering green port initiatives.
• Significant advancements were reported in developing mega-ports, improving cargo handling efficiency, and increasing the share of renewable energy in port operations.
• Challenges related to attracting private investment, streamlining regulatory processes, and developing skilled manpower for the maritime sector were discussed, with new strategies proposed.
• The vision is crucial for India's economic growth, trade competitiveness, and achieving the 'Blue Economy' potential.

articles.headline: Ministry of Finance Enhances Gender Budgeting Initiative with Focus on Outcome-Based Allocation
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Finance has announced significant enhancements to India's Gender Budgeting (GB) initiative, shifting towards a more outcome-based allocation and monitoring framework.
• The updated guidelines require ministries and departments to quantify the gender impact of their schemes and programs, moving beyond mere expenditure tracking to assess actual benefits for women and girls.
• A new digital dashboard will be launched to provide real-time data on gender-disaggregated outcomes, improving transparency and accountability in GB implementation.
• Focus areas include women's economic empowerment, health, education, and safety, with a special emphasis on schemes addressing the digital gender divide and climate change impacts on women.
• This move aims to make Gender Budgeting a more effective tool for achieving gender equality and inclusive development goals.

articles.headline: India-UK Free Trade Agreement Negotiations Enter Final Stages
articles.explainer:
• Negotiations for the India-United Kingdom Free Trade Agreement (FTA) are reportedly in their final stages, with both sides expressing optimism about an early conclusion.
• Key areas of discussion include market access for goods and services, intellectual property rights, investment protection, and digital trade.
• India is seeking greater mobility for its professionals, while the UK is keen on reducing tariffs on Scotch whisky, automobiles, and financial services.
• The FTA is expected to significantly boost bilateral trade and investment, creating new opportunities for businesses and consumers in both countries.
• Despite some persistent sticking points, political will from both governments is driving efforts to resolve outstanding issues and finalize the agreement.

articles.headline: National Supercomputing Mission Enters Phase 3, Boosting AI and Data Science Research
articles.explainer:
• The National Supercomputing Mission (NSM) has successfully entered its third phase, significantly expanding India's supercomputing capabilities for advanced research and development.
• Phase 3 focuses on deploying more powerful indigenous supercomputers with enhanced processing speeds, specifically tailored for Artificial Intelligence (AI), Machine Learning (ML), and Big Data analytics.
• The mission aims to establish a robust national supercomputing grid, connecting various academic and research institutions, to facilitate cutting-edge research in diverse fields like climate modeling, drug discovery, and cybersecurity.
• This expansion will provide researchers with unprecedented computational power, enabling them to tackle complex scientific challenges and foster innovation.
• NSM is a joint initiative of the Department of Science and Technology (DST) and the Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY).

articles.headline: Ministry of Education Reviews Progress of National Education Policy 2020 Implementation
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Education conducted a comprehensive review meeting to assess the progress made in implementing the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 across all levels of education.
• Significant strides were noted in integrating vocational education, promoting multilingualism, and establishing the National Research Foundation (NRF) to boost research.
• Challenges identified include ensuring equitable access to digital learning resources, capacity building for teachers in new pedagogical approaches, and harmonizing state-level curricula with NEP guidelines.
• The review emphasized the need for accelerated efforts in foundational literacy and numeracy (FLN) and strengthening early childhood care and education (ECCE).
• NEP 2020 aims to transform India's education system to meet the demands of the 21st century, focusing on holistic, multidisciplinary, and skill-based learning.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to the 'Atmanirbhar Krishi Abhiyan 2.0' recently launched by the government, consider the following statements:
1. It aims to reduce agricultural imports and promote self-sufficiency in food grains only.
2. It focuses on integrating advanced technologies like AI and blockchain for supply chain management.
3. The scheme includes establishing 'Agri-Export Zones' (AEZs) and dedicated logistics corridors.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
options: (a) 1 and 2 only
(b) 2 and 3 only
(c) 1 and 3 only
(d) 1, 2 and 3
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is incorrect. The Abhiyan aims to boost agricultural exports and farmer income, not solely reduce imports and focus on food grains. It promotes value addition and global market access. Statement 2 is correct, as the initiative focuses on integrating advanced technologies like AI and blockchain for efficient supply chain management. Statement 3 is also correct, as a key component is the establishment of 'Agri-Export Zones' (AEZs) and dedicated logistics corridors for exports.

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following statements best describes the primary objective of the Supreme Court committee's recommendation for AI integration in the judiciary?
options: (a) To replace human judges with AI systems for all legal judgments.
(b) To assist judges in administrative tasks and case management to reduce pendency.
(c) To allow litigants to resolve disputes directly through AI platforms without court intervention.
(d) To automate the entire legal research process, making lawyers redundant.
prelimsMocks.answer: (b) To assist judges in administrative tasks and case management to reduce pendency.
prelimsMocks.explanation: The Supreme Court-appointed committee recommended AI as an assistive tool for judges, primarily for case management, identifying precedents, automating administrative tasks, and transcribing proceedings, with the aim of reducing pendency and improving efficiency. It explicitly stated that AI would not replace human judgment. Options (a), (c), and (d) represent extreme or incorrect interpretations of the committee's recommendations.

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following pairs regarding India's space missions:
1. Mangalyaan: Mars Orbiter Mission
2. Chandrayaan-3: Lunar Lander Mission
3. Shukrayaan-2: Jupiter Orbiter Mission
How many of the pairs given above are correctly matched?
options: (a) Only one
(b) Only two
(c) All three
(d) None
prelimsMocks.explanation: Pair 1 (Mangalyaan: Mars Orbiter Mission) is correctly matched. Mangalyaan was India's first interplanetary mission to Mars. Pair 2 (Chandrayaan-3: Lunar Lander Mission) is correctly matched. Chandrayaan-3 successfully landed on the Moon in 2023. Pair 3 (Shukrayaan-2: Jupiter Orbiter Mission) is incorrectly matched. Shukrayaan-2 is India's upcoming mission to Venus, not Jupiter.

prelimsMocks.question: The term 'Urban Heat Island' (UHI) effect, as highlighted by a recent ISRO study, primarily refers to:
options: (a) The phenomenon of increased seismic activity in urban areas due to construction.
(b) The higher temperatures observed in urban areas compared to surrounding rural areas.
(c) The concentration of atmospheric pollutants forming a 'dome' over cities.
(d) The depletion of groundwater levels in metropolitan regions.
prelimsMocks.answer: (b) The higher temperatures observed in urban areas compared to surrounding rural areas.
prelimsMocks.explanation: The Urban Heat Island (UHI) effect is a phenomenon where urban areas experience higher temperatures than their surrounding rural areas, primarily due to factors like rapid urbanization, reduced green cover, and heat-absorbing construction materials. The ISRO study specifically focused on this temperature differential.

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following is NOT a proposed amendment by the Election Commission of India (ECI) to the Electoral Bonds Scheme?
options: (a) Mandating disclosure of purchaser identity to the ECI.
(b) Increasing the anonymity period for electoral bonds.
(c) Increasing the frequency of electoral bond issuance windows.
(d) Reducing the anonymity period for electoral bonds.
prelimsMocks.answer: (b) Increasing the anonymity period for electoral bonds.
prelimsMocks.explanation: The ECI's proposals aim to enhance transparency. Therefore, increasing the anonymity period (option b) would contradict this objective. The ECI proposed to 'reduce the anonymity period' (option d), not increase it. Options (a) and (c) are correctly stated as proposed amendments.

mainsMocks.approach: Briefly introduce the Indus Waters Treaty (IWT) as a landmark agreement between India and Pakistan (1960), brokered by the World Bank, highlighting its resilience. GS1.headline: Archaeological Survey of India Unearths Ancient Buddhist Monastery in Odisha
GS1.explainer:
• The Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) has announced the discovery of a sprawling ancient Buddhist monastic complex during excavations in the Jajpur district of Odisha.
• The site, believed to date back to the 7th-10th century CE, includes stupas, meditation cells, intricate sculptures of Buddhist deities, and a well-preserved prayer hall.
• This discovery sheds new light on the spread of Vajrayana Buddhism in Eastern India and the rich cultural heritage of ancient Kalinga.
• Artifacts recovered from the site, including terracotta seals and pottery, provide valuable insights into the daily life and religious practices of the monastic community.
• The ASI plans further excavations and conservation efforts, aiming to develop the site as a major heritage tourism destination and a center for Buddhist studies.

GS2.headline: Supreme Court Committee Recommends AI Integration for Faster Dispute Resolution in Lower Courts
GS2.explainer:
• A Supreme Court-appointed committee has submitted a report recommending the phased integration of Artificial Intelligence (AI) tools to assist in dispute resolution processes, particularly in district and subordinate courts.
• The recommendations include using AI for case management, identifying similar precedents, automating administrative tasks, and transcribing court proceedings, aiming to reduce pendency.
• The committee emphasized that AI would serve as an assistive tool for judges and not replace human judgment, focusing on improving efficiency and access to justice.
• Concerns regarding data privacy, algorithmic bias, and digital divide were also addressed, with proposals for robust data protection frameworks and ethical AI guidelines.
• The report suggests pilot projects in select high courts and district courts before a nationwide rollout, along with extensive training programs for judicial officers and legal professionals.

• The exercise included advanced anti-submarine warfare drills, maritime surveillance operations, search and rescue scenarios, and cross-deck flying operations.
• Both navies exchanged best practices in maritime security, disaster relief, and humanitarian assistance operations, reinforcing regional stability.
• The exercise underscored the strong maritime security cooperation between the two nations and their commitment to a free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific region.
• Senior naval officials from both countries reiterated their resolve to counter common maritime threats, including piracy, illegal fishing, and drug trafficking.

GS2.headline: India Hosts G20+ Summit on Global Digital Public Infrastructure Framework
GS2.explainer:
• India is hosting a special G20+ summit focused on establishing a global framework for Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI), building on the successful outcomes of its G20 presidency.
• The summit aims to standardize protocols and foster interoperability for DPIs like digital identity, payments, and data exchange across member nations, promoting inclusive digital transformation globally.
• Discussions include financing mechanisms for DPI adoption in developing countries, capacity building, and addressing concerns related to data governance and cross-border data flows.
• India's experience with Aadhaar, UPI, and other DPIs is being showcased as a model for scalable and inclusive digital solutions.
• The initiative seeks to bridge the global digital divide and enable equitable access to digital services for billions worldwide.

GS2.headline: Government Launches 'Saksham Yuva' Skill Development Program for Rural Youth
GS2.explainer:
• The Ministry of Skill Development and Entrepreneurship, in collaboration with state governments, has launched the 'Saksham Yuva' program, a new initiative aimed at empowering rural youth through skill development and vocational training.
• The program focuses on demand-driven skills relevant to local economies, including renewable energy installation, digital literacy, sustainable agriculture practices, and rural tourism.
• It integrates apprenticeship opportunities with industry partners and provides financial assistance for entrepreneurship, encouraging self-employment among beneficiaries.
• 'Saksham Yuva' seeks to bridge the skill gap in rural areas, enhance employability, and reduce distress migration to urban centers.
• The initiative includes provisions for special training modules for women and marginalized communities, ensuring inclusive growth and gender equality in skill development.

GS2.headline: Election Commission Proposes Amendments to Enhance Transparency in Electoral Bonds Scheme
GS2.explainer:
• The Election Commission of India (ECI) has submitted a proposal to the Law Ministry suggesting amendments to the Electoral Bonds Scheme to enhance transparency and accountability in political funding.
• Key recommendations include mandating disclosure of the identity of electoral bond purchasers to the ECI, while maintaining anonymity for political parties.
• The ECI also proposed reducing the anonymity period for the bonds and increasing the frequency of their issuance windows to ensure more regular and transparent funding cycles.
• These proposals aim to address long-standing concerns raised by civil society organizations and various political parties regarding the opacity of the current scheme.
• The suggested changes are intended to strike a balance between donor privacy and public right to information regarding political funding.

GS2.headline: India Calls for Review of Indus Waters Treaty Provisions Amidst Climate Change Impacts
GS2.explainer:
• India has formally called for a review of certain provisions of the Indus Waters Treaty (IWT) with Pakistan, citing the changing hydrological conditions due to climate change and increasing water demands.
• The Ministry of Water Resources highlighted that the existing treaty, signed in 1960, may not adequately address contemporary challenges such as glacial melt, altered river flows, and growing population needs.
• India seeks to explore mechanisms for better water management, flood control, and optimal utilization of western river waters allocated to Pakistan, while respecting the spirit of the treaty.
• This move follows ongoing expert-level discussions and aims to initiate a constructive dialogue with Pakistan on updating the treaty to reflect current realities.
• The IWT, brokered by the World Bank, has largely remained resilient despite political tensions, governing the sharing of six rivers between the two nations.

GS2.trivia: The Indus Waters Treaty is considered one of the most successful water-sharing treaties in the world, having survived multiple conflicts between India and Pakistan.

GS2.headline: Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission Reports Accelerated Adoption of Digital Health IDs
GS2.explainer:
• The National Health Authority (NHA) announced a significant surge in the creation of Ayushman Bharat Health Accounts (ABHA IDs), indicating accelerated adoption of the Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM).
• Over 500 million unique digital health IDs have now been generated, enabling citizens to link their health records digitally and access various healthcare services.
• The mission aims to create a seamless online platform for interoperability of health data, improving efficiency, transparency, and accessibility of healthcare across India.
• Integration with public and private healthcare providers, including hospitals, clinics, and diagnostic labs, is expanding rapidly, forming a robust digital health ecosystem.
• The increased adoption is attributed to widespread awareness campaigns and the ease of creating ABHA IDs through various online and offline channels, including government health programs.

GS2.headline: Ministry of Finance Enhances Gender Budgeting Initiative with Focus on Outcome-Based Allocation
GS2.explainer:
• The Ministry of Finance has announced significant enhancements to India's Gender Budgeting (GB) initiative, shifting towards a more outcome-based allocation and monitoring framework.
• The updated guidelines require ministries and departments to quantify the gender impact of their schemes and programs, moving beyond mere expenditure tracking to assess actual benefits for women and girls.
• A new digital dashboard will be launched to provide real-time data on gender-disaggregated outcomes, improving transparency and accountability in GB implementation.
• Focus areas include women's economic empowerment, health, education, and safety, with a special emphasis on schemes addressing the digital gender divide and climate change impacts on women.
• This move aims to make Gender Budgeting a more effective tool for achieving gender equality and inclusive development goals.

GS2.headline: India-UK Free Trade Agreement Negotiations Enter Final Stages
GS2.explainer:
• Negotiations for the India-United Kingdom Free Trade Agreement (FTA) are reportedly in their final stages, with both sides expressing optimism about an early conclusion.
• Key areas of discussion include market access for goods and services, intellectual property rights, investment protection, and digital trade.
• India is seeking greater mobility for its professionals, while the UK is keen on reducing tariffs on Scotch whisky, automobiles, and financial services.
• The FTA is expected to significantly boost bilateral trade and investment, creating new opportunities for businesses and consumers in both countries.
• Despite some persistent sticking points, political will from both governments is driving efforts to resolve outstanding issues and finalize the agreement.

GS2.headline: Ministry of Education Reviews Progress of National Education Policy 2020 Implementation
GS2.explainer:
• The Ministry of Education conducted a comprehensive review meeting to assess the progress made in implementing the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 across all levels of education.
• Significant strides were noted in integrating vocational education, promoting multilingualism, and establishing the National Research Foundation (NRF) to boost research.
• Challenges identified include ensuring equitable access to digital learning resources, capacity building for teachers in new pedagogical approaches, and harmonizing state-level curricula with NEP guidelines.
• The review emphasized the need for accelerated efforts in foundational literacy and numeracy (FLN) and strengthening early childhood care and education (ECCE).
• NEP 2020 aims to transform India's education system to meet the demands of the 21st century, focusing on holistic, multidisciplinary, and skill-based learning.

GS3.headline: Government Unveils 'Atmanirbhar Krishi Abhiyan 2.0' to Boost Agricultural Exports and Farmer Income
GS3.explainer:
• The Union Agriculture Ministry launched 'Atmanirbhar Krishi Abhiyan 2.0', a comprehensive policy framework aimed at enhancing agricultural productivity, promoting value addition, and increasing farmer incomes.
• The initiative focuses on integrating advanced technologies like AI and blockchain for supply chain management, improving access to credit for farmers, and strengthening agricultural infrastructure.
• A key component is the establishment of 'Agri-Export Zones' (AEZs) and dedicated logistics corridors to facilitate seamless export of Indian agricultural produce to global markets.
• The program also includes provisions for skill development and capacity building among farmers, particularly in sustainable farming practices and digital literacy.
• It aims to reduce post-harvest losses significantly and ensure better price realization for farmers by connecting them directly to national and international buyers.

GS3.headline: National Quantum Mission Reports Significant Progress in Quantum Computing and Communication
GS3.explainer:
• The Department of Science & Technology (DST) released a progress report on the National Quantum Mission (NQM), highlighting advancements in developing quantum computing platforms and secure quantum communication networks.
• Researchers have achieved milestones in building indigenous quantum processors, with promising results in error correction and qubit stability, crucial for practical quantum applications.
• The mission has also made strides in quantum key distribution (QKD) technology, aiming to establish secure communication links for critical infrastructure and defense.
• Collaborative projects between academic institutions, industry partners, and government labs are accelerating research and development, fostering a robust quantum ecosystem in India.
• The NQM is positioning India as a global leader in quantum technologies, with a focus on both fundamental research and application development in areas like healthcare, finance, and defense.

GS3.headline: ISRO Study Highlights Worsening Urban Heat Island Effect in Indian Cities
GS3.explainer:
• A new study by the Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) utilizing satellite imagery and climate models has revealed a significant increase in the Urban Heat Island (UHI) effect across major Indian cities.
• The research indicates that rapid urbanization, reduction in green cover, and extensive use of heat-absorbing construction materials are primary contributors to higher temperatures in urban areas compared to their rural surroundings.
• Cities like Delhi, Mumbai, and Chennai are experiencing UHI intensities ranging from 3-7°C, leading to increased energy consumption for cooling, adverse health impacts, and exacerbated air pollution.
• The study emphasizes the urgent need for urban planning strategies that incorporate green infrastructure, cool roofs, and sustainable building materials to mitigate the UHI effect.
• ISRO's findings provide crucial data for policymakers to develop targeted interventions for climate resilience and sustainable urban development.

GS3.headline: National Policy on Climate Resilience and Adaptation Unveiled, Focus on Vulnerable Regions
GS3.explainer:
• The Union Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change has launched a comprehensive 'National Policy on Climate Resilience and Adaptation' to address the growing impacts of climate change in India.
• The policy prioritizes vulnerable regions and communities, focusing on building climate-resilient infrastructure, promoting sustainable agriculture, and strengthening early warning systems for extreme weather events.
• Key strategies include mainstreaming climate adaptation into development planning, enhancing ecosystem-based adaptation, and mobilizing climate finance from domestic and international sources.
• It emphasizes multi-stakeholder collaboration, involving state governments, local bodies, civil society, and the private sector, for effective implementation.
• The policy aims to reduce climate-related risks, protect livelihoods, and ensure sustainable development in the face of a changing climate.

GS3.headline: ISRO Gears Up for Venus Orbiter Mission 'Shukrayaan-2' Launch Window
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) has announced that preparations are in full swing for the 'Shukrayaan-2' mission, India's second planetary mission to Venus.
• The mission aims to study Venus's atmosphere, surface, and subsurface, focusing on its geological processes, atmospheric composition, and the presence of any active volcanism.
• Shukrayaan-2 will carry advanced scientific payloads, including a synthetic aperture radar (SAR) for high-resolution mapping of the Venusian surface and instruments to analyze atmospheric gases.
• The launch window is anticipated in the latter half of 2026, leveraging optimal planetary alignment for a cost-effective trajectory.
• This mission reinforces India's growing capabilities in deep space exploration and its commitment to contributing to global planetary science research.

GS3.headline: NITI Aayog Proposes Comprehensive Circular Economy Policy Framework
GS3.explainer:
• NITI Aayog has released a draft 'National Circular Economy Policy Framework', aimed at promoting resource efficiency, waste reduction, and sustainable consumption and production patterns across various sectors.
• The framework identifies key sectors for intervention, including electronics, batteries, vehicles, packaging, and construction & demolition waste, proposing specific strategies for each.
• It emphasizes Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR), fostering innovation in recycling technologies, and creating market demand for recycled products.
• The policy aims to reduce India's reliance on virgin resources, mitigate environmental pollution, and create new economic opportunities in the recycling and reuse sectors.
• NITI Aayog is seeking stakeholder feedback on the draft, with a vision to integrate circular economy principles into national development planning.

GS3.headline: RBI Report Highlights Progress in Financial Inclusion, Focus on Digital Payments
GS3.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) has released its annual 'Financial Inclusion Index' report, indicating continued progress in expanding access to financial services across India, particularly through digital channels.
• The report notes significant improvements in indicators such as banking penetration, credit availability to underserved sectors, and the widespread adoption of UPI and other digital payment platforms.
• While urban areas show high financial literacy and service utilization, the report highlights the need for targeted interventions to improve financial inclusion in remote rural areas and among marginalized communities.
• RBI emphasizes the role of FinTech innovations, Business Correspondents (BCs), and financial literacy campaigns in driving further inclusion.
• The central bank reiterated its commitment to fostering an inclusive financial ecosystem that supports equitable economic growth and poverty reduction.

GS3.headline: Conservation Efforts Boost Ganges River Dolphin Population in Key Habitats
GS3.explainer:
• Recent surveys conducted by the Wildlife Institute of India (WII) and state forest departments indicate a positive trend in the population of the endangered Ganges River Dolphin in several protected areas.
• Increased enforcement against illegal fishing, reduced pollution levels in critical stretches of the Ganges and its tributaries, and community engagement in conservation efforts are cited as primary reasons for the rebound.
• The 'Project Dolphin' initiative, launched by the central government, has played a crucial role in coordinating these conservation activities, including habitat restoration and awareness campaigns.
• Despite progress, challenges remain from habitat fragmentation due to dams, plastic pollution, and occasional accidental deaths from fishing gear, necessitating continuous monitoring and intervention.
• The Ganges River Dolphin, India's National Aquatic Animal, serves as an indicator species for the health of the riverine ecosystem.

GS3.headline: Government Amends E-Waste Management Rules to Strengthen Collection and Recycling Targets
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change has notified amendments to the E-Waste (Management) Rules, 2022, introducing stricter targets and expanding the scope for collection and recycling of electronic waste.
• The revised rules mandate higher Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR) obligations for manufacturers and importers, requiring them to collect and recycle a larger percentage of their products.
• A new digital portal for tracking e-waste generation and processing has been launched, aiming for greater transparency and accountability across the value chain.
• The amendments also include provisions for promoting refurbishment and reuse of electronic products, thereby extending their lifecycle and reducing the burden on raw materials.
• These measures are expected to formalize the informal e-waste sector, create green jobs, and mitigate the environmental and health hazards associated with improper e-waste disposal.

GS3.headline: National Supercomputing Mission Enters Phase 3, Boosting AI and Data Science Research
GS3.explainer:
• The National Supercomputing Mission (NSM) has successfully entered its third phase, significantly expanding India's supercomputing capabilities for advanced research and development.
• Phase 3 focuses on deploying more powerful indigenous supercomputers with enhanced processing speeds, specifically tailored for Artificial Intelligence (AI), Machine Learning (ML), and Big Data analytics.
• The mission aims to establish a robust national supercomputing grid, connecting various academic and research institutions, to facilitate cutting-edge research in diverse fields like climate modeling, drug discovery, and cybersecurity.
• This expansion will provide researchers with unprecedented computational power, enabling them to tackle complex scientific challenges and foster innovation.
• NSM is a joint initiative of the Department of Science and Technology (DST) and the Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY).
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
GS3.source: Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY)
prelimsPoints: National Supercomputing Mission (NSM) enters Phase 3.
Focus on deploying powerful indigenous supercomputers for AI, ML, Big Data analytics.
Aims to establish a national supercomputing grid connecting institutions.
Joint initiative of DST and MeitY.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of supercomputing capabilities for India's scientific research, technological advancement, and national security.
Analyze how the National Supercomputing Mission contributes to fostering innovation in Artificial Intelligence, Machine Learning, and data science.
Evaluate the challenges and opportunities in building and maintaining a robust national supercomputing infrastructure in India.
Examine the potential applications of enhanced supercomputing power across various sectors, from climate modeling to drug discovery.
GS3.imageDescription: A complex network of supercomputer servers, glowing with digital data streams.
GS3.trivia: PARAM Shivay, India's first supercomputer assembled indigenously under the NSM, was installed at IIT-BHU in 2019.
